problems.label: 1a
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.

problems.label: 1b
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.
problems.display: You have to be at least 18 years of age.

problems.label: 1c
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.
problems.display: There are more than 10 girls on the playground.

problems.label: 1d
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.
problems.display: There are at least a dozen windows.

problems.label: 1e
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.

problems.label: 1f
problems.group: 1. Write an inequality for each phrase. You will need to choose a variable to represent the quantity in question.
problems.display: The club is for children who are 12 years old or younger.

problems.label: 2a
problems.group: 2. Make up a situation from real life that could be described by the given inequality.

answer.hint: Answers will vary. Example: You have to be less than ten to join the club.

problems.label: 2b
problems.group: 2. Make up a situation from real life that could be described by the given inequality.

answer.hint: Answers will vary. Example: This game is suitable for age seven or older.

problems.label: 2c
problems.group: 2. Make up a situation from real life that could be described by the given inequality.

answer.hint: Answers will vary. Example: The price has to be set at more than $200.

problems.label: 2d
problems.group: 2. Make up a situation from real life that could be described by the given inequality.

answer.hint: Answers will vary. Example: You have to weigh not more than 40 kilograms to jump on the trampoline.

problems.label: 2e
problems.group: 2. Make up a situation from real life that could be described by the given inequality.

answer.hint: Answers will vary. Example: He wants to buy at least 2000 kilograms of hay.

problems.label: 6a

problems.label: 6b
